---
title: "Tommaso Maccaferri"
about:
  template: jolla
  image: "Tommaso Maccaferri Portait Photo.jpeg"
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/tommaso-maccaferri-6bba44262/
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/Tommaso-Maccaferri
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:tommaso.maccaferri@gmail.com
---

## Welcome

Research Master's student in **Economic and Financial Research** at **Maastricht University**, specializing in advanced econometric modeling, quantitative historical analysis, and policy evaluation. Previously graduated *Cum Laude* in Economics from the **University of Bologna**.

I combine rigorous economic theory with computational methods to tackle complex real-world problems using **R**, **Stata**, **Julia**, and **C++**.

## Research Interests

- **Labor Economics**: Trade shocks, local labor markets, employment dynamics
- **Monetary Policy and Banking**: Financial markets, macroprudential regulation
- **Economic History**: Postwar reconstruction, growth accounting, historical data analysis
- **Applied Econometrics**: Causal inference, panel data, shift-share designs, replication studies

## Current Focus

My recent work includes rigorous replication studies of seminal papers in trade economics and growth accounting analyses of historical economic miracles. I'm particularly interested in how large economic shocks—trade, technology, migration—affect local labor markets and their long-term effects on regional development.

I believe in **reproducible research**: all my code and data are publicly available on GitHub, and I'm committed to methodological rigor and transparent analysis.

---

*Explore my [Projects](projects.qmd) to see detailed examples of my research work, or check out my [CV & Bio](about.qmd) for more information about my academic background, professional experience, and skills.*
